Just as a precursor to the entire event, this is how I take notes at every single event that I go to. So I just want your time to be best used. And this is the method that I have used after going to lots of these types of things. And so when I'm listening, I will just write down the ideas that come to mind so I don't lose them. During the breaks at night or sometimes even if it's worst case scenario, during the flight's home, I'll go through all of my notes. I'll prioritize the items that are important on a new page. So I'll take everything, rewrite it on a new page in order, and then I'll draw an arrow and write down the actions according to that prioritized item. So let's say I wanted to have implemented new, like the presale questionnaire. So I'd be like presale questionnaire, and then the next day I'd be like, that's the first thing I want to implement so that'd be number one. And then the action is like go to this website, download it, and then swap logo and print. Like so that way I know what I have to do. So when I get home, I only have my list of actions, not like things I have to think about. And that way, and then after that, the fourth step is I just put it on my calendar for when I'm going to do it, and then I just do it. And so that's how I break down all of this information into how do I make my business make more money. Does that make sense? Cool. Okay. So let's get right into it. There's a ton I want to share. Clarity of path, the one number that rules them all. So at the last summit, my first presentation was about how I realized that the only thing that can really be given at an event like this is conviction. Because the modules are for teaching and for taking you through how to set up Facebook ad and a pixel and blah, blah, blah, blah. So I really thought a lot about what can we give at a summit that I'm not already giving with the modules. And so I realized that besides shattering false beliefs, which was what I came into, the last event with the intention of doing, yes, you can sell something for $3,000. You can do it and showing a whole bunch of people who had. But as I thought about this one, I realized that there's something that can be delivered of extreme value, which is clarity. And so when I pay for consulting days with Charfin and some of the other people that I pay for consulting days for, usually like $30,000 to $50,000 a day, which is kind of insane now that we think about it, I don't go there to learn a skill. We have the skills. I go there to know what not to do. Here's all the different opportunities that we have. How do I prioritize them? What's the one thing we need to be focused on for the next 90 days? And that's what I go to those types of meetings for so that I can bounce ideas off of someone so that I can get that clarity. The beauty is that with this path, where you have people who have already treaded, like right now I don't have any other company that's done more in this space than I have that I can model. And I don't really want to model them anyways. In the gym space, there are lots of gyms right now who are hitting a million in this room who you can model. And I'm just going to show you the path of what they did so that you can do it too. That sound cool? Okay. So I got thinking, yes, we'll deliver the conviction for those of you who are new. This is new first time. Woo! Welcome. So we're going to try and give you guys the conviction that yes, you can sell lots more expensive stuff and you can make more than $100,000 a month from one location. You don't have to have 10 locations to make that kind of money. But in order to get there, you need to have a clear path and one thing to focus on, which as a side note, oh yeah, see, I know what I'm going to say. And that's something that everybody can leave here with because it was one of the main takeaways that I had from the seven-figure gym. So I did round tables with about half of the seven-figure gyms that we have just to just ask questions and kind of sift through all the information to try and come up with the clear things that they're all doing, the commonalities, not the differences, but the commonalities. And I would say the single biggest takeaway, or none, was that they just focused on doing the same thing over and over again. Really sexy, right? That's going to give you some mind-blowing thing. And the thing is, it isn't sexy. Everything makes you money. You get better at doing stuff that you're already doing and just do it better. And a lot of times, making your restaurants' food tastes better will make you more money. It's just not sexy. I love to put a new ordering system in, but the food just has to taste good. So I set the table with that intention, so let me ask you this question. Who here signed up to be an entrepreneur to be free? I mean, I did. And so if you look at the different types of freedom, you have financial freedom, geographic freedom, temporal freedom, which is basically be wherever you want to be geographically, whenever you want to be, or sorry, whenever you want, and do whatever you want. So you have all three aspects of that freedom. And what's interesting is that if that's true, then why are most people who are entrepreneurs not financially free, geographically free, or temporarily free? In fact, usually they're far more chained down than they were when they just had a job that was nine to five. It's kind of interesting, right? You're like, I'm going to be an entrepreneur, and then you're like, I'm going to take all my savings and all my security and dump it into this thing and then have to be there 18 hours a day and never take a vacation. You're like, I want freedom. It's kind of funny. I mean, I know because I did the same thing. And the reason for that is because we're running the wrong model, right? That's why so many of you guys have kind of come into our world recently, especially people who raised your hands. You've seen how quickly your business can change if you're applying the right systems, right? And so that's the first part. The second part is that if you have the right model, you need to have the right mindset about it. And so we're going to be covering both of those in action specs this weekend. And I'll be covering the model side because that's kind of more my strong suit. And Layla is actually me paying a far more prominent role this weekend. Who's happy about that, right? And she will, yeah, I know, she's the best. And Layla will be covering all the mindset stuff. I don't like the term mindset, but it's like you need to be the leader that can lead the gym that you don't have yet, if that makes sense. And she's like the single best example of that. So here's something that will hopefully ring true for you, the thing that separates the rich from the poor. And that's knowledge and execution. And consistent execution, excuse me. And most of you guys now should have the knowledge of what you're supposed to do, which means that something's getting in the way from the execution standpoint. And that's where clarity comes in. Is that you should just know what you're supposed to do so that nothing else gets in the way of that. So my goal here is to give you the clarity to achieve the freedom that you actually signed up for when you decided to open your first gym. Okay. And with that clarity, it'll protect you from taking out loans for new ventures. I get messages all the time from you guys who are like, hey, I've got this cool thing for solar. Or like, I've got like, dude, just run your gym. Taking out new business partners when you don't need to. Hey, this client of mine is really, really rich and he just wants to give me 50 grand. That sounds like a terrible idea. Like banks will give you money too. Like it doesn't mean you need to take it. Buying more equipment unnecessarily. Hey, we just made a bunch of money in this challenge. So I'm thinking about redoing my facility. Why you just made money? Like just take it and just do it again. That's all you need to do. Hey, you know, we were totally in debt. We haven't made money for a year and a half and we finally paid our debt off. So I'm going to open up a second location tomorrow because now I'm debt free. Like I'm telling you, like I get all the time, all the time. And then, you know, another widget slash distraction that takes you away from your goal. Right. All that is just not having clarity. The thing is, the better you get, the better the opportunities you have to say no to. I cannot tell you how many people have come up to me and been like, dude, let me partner you. Let's let's model Jim launch with chiropractors. Like and they're like, they've got a decent model or whatever. And I'm like, no, like I could. I don't, you know what I mean? Because like, well, what's the goal? Well, to go make money and do it. I'm like, well, I'm making money. So I don't need to do that. I'm just going to keep doing this. Right. And then just you get more and more disciplined about it. Okay. So because at the end of the day, a crystal clear path will make your life so much easier and it will give you the strength to say no, which is like the hardest thing in the world as an entrepreneur. Because we just love everything. And so I always ask myself, is this thing that I'm looking at or even putting my attention to really something that's going to drive me towards my desired critical number? Or is it a distraction or a nice to have? Right. And most times, if I'm like, is this going to get more Jim since Jim launch? Then it's probably a distraction. Almost like 99 times out of 100. So it makes me make decision making for me a lot easier. And so the often I want to share with you guys in this presentation and what clarity in the Jim business model looks like. So some of you guys have seen this before, but this was like mind blowing for me to write down because after making everything that was in legacy 1.0 and legacy 2.0, we're releasing in chunks mostly because I realized that if I released everything at once, no one could go through it all. So we're releasing it like every week now. Kind of like putting it in with all the updates. But if you really think about how this model has now evolved, if you see the 12-week challenge, that's for HTTP. 90% of you or 85% of you don't even need to think about that because you're not close to full capacity. You haven't gotten how to run a challenge up. Your fulfillment is not on point. Your retention sucks, to be honest with you. And so you just need to focus on doing those things better. So if you ignore the 12-week challenge, then the only thing is you have an entrance point to your business, which is a six-week challenge. Notice the commonalities. I have gym lunch. All right. And then you have your raw portal, your internal promotions, the things that you're doing to keep them engaged and get them excited as they go through this journey with you, right? Get them excited at events, et cetera. And then underneath of that, the backbone that supports the whole thing is your culture and your team training. Isn't that like, is that like simpler to everyone? Like, I got to get good at this thing that gets them in, and then I get really good at this thing that keeps them here. And the thing that keeps them here is how well I treat my people so that they treat them well so they stay. That make sense? Like, does that simplify things a little bit? Okay. For me, it simplifies it when I see it like that. Okay. Which is also why I read me legacy two-point note of look like this. So it's like, choose your adventure. Most people are going to go six-week challenge. That's what you should be doing. And then you have the things that are member monetization, things like internal plays, bring a friend challenge, referrals, retention, et cetera. So we just reverse engineer our own success. So we want 750 gyms in legacy by 2019. And I want you, like, we're going to do this with your, the gyms in a second. I just want you to follow along. And so that means that we need 350 more than now. Okay. And that's it, which means right now we convert about 50 a month into legacy. And if we convert 60% of our launches into legacy, then we need 20 launches a week. And so the only action that I have to get to that big long-term thing is I need to get 14 applications, which means I need to create and optimize as weekly. That's it. Does that make sense? Okay. But like the actual, like, I would love to do a million other things, but this is our goal and that's what I want to do. And so, like, three quarters of the time, it's me just tying my hands together and like shutting my mouth. And just being like, okay, there's six today. You have to get into it because like if you set these huge goals, how do you think you're going to get there? It's just one or two a day every single day over and over and over and over again. And it will bore you senseless. And you should just be focusing on just getting really good at doing that. And so, I think I covered this. But the first truth about this is that if you're someone who really like does jump to like doesn't have the discipline yet to like say no to all these other things, it's just a sign that you're a new entrepreneur. And I'm not saying that it's like as a harsh thing. It's just true. Usually it's like seeker style behavior is to like read a book about this and get really excited and then read a book about this and get really excited and then listen to a podcast and be like, I need to go on YouTube or, you know, whatever, right? And just jump from thing to thing to thing rather than being able to manage yourself and like truly like direct your focus. Warren Buffett and Bill Gates were asked in an interview at the same time to write down the number one thing that had been responsible for the billions that they had made. And they both wrote down on a card and then they flipped it over at the same time and it was the same word, focus. That was it. Just doing one thing, right? Just doing it over and over again. So truth two, am I driving this home? The more boring you make your business, the more money you will make. Because if you don't have tons of fires, if you don't have tons of stuff to do, it means stuff's going well. It's supposed to happen, right? Sometimes we go and break stuff to have stuff to do. Who's done that? Right? So this was October of last year. I got kicked out of my own company. Straight up. This is a gym watch. I didn't go through the comm cycle and just reached out to one of our customer support reps and was like, hey, I need you to do this and this. And then that person went to Mimi, who's our COO, and was like, oh my god, like in tears. I can't believe you do this. I have all these other responsibilities. How am I supposed to do this and what Alex told me to do? My bad. But after that point, I've been isolated. I run a team of three people. That's it. I'm like in this bubble. They don't let me talk to anyone. And then Layla is president of the entire rest of the company, which I find humorous. And don't say, well, I'm going to be cutting this out and cutting that out. It's like, no, just like what is. So if this is what it currently is right now, period, then all you need to do after that, and if you're including your payroll on that, then you can add whatever else you need to add on top for to add up to 15k. If you're not including your payroll on that, then you would add everything that you need to get your 15 grand from this number. Step two. Okay. In this example, I had 3000 is you for payroll. So like if you have yourself on payroll right now at 3000 a month, which is what I would recommend most of you guys do, just to keep the tax man away and not like saying that you're trying to skip payroll taxes and Medicare and all that kind of stuff. Usually most people can start at 30, 36 a year and no one's going to bug you if you take dividends that are higher than that at a lower tax rate. So that's the example I'm using. So if you want to get to 12,000 to get to 15 total, then you need another 60 EFTs to hit your revenue goal. Does that make sense? So step three is you have step one and two together to get your total EFT goal. And then that gets us to the number that we need, right? So there you go. Clearly it's not. So 174. Actually I did the math wrong here. I did it right on stage. All right. So yeah, I should say 184, right? And then that gets you to your EFT goal. Does that seem more doable? Like this is what is, here are all of my expenses. This is how much, how many EFTs I need to just cover my expenses and this is how many EFTs I need to actually take home the money that I want to take home. And then don't do anything else. Just do that. Okay. So in case you didn't pull out your statement in the real expense totals, you can do this part as a hypothetical projection to know when you're going to actually hit it. Okay. I would put this critical number that you just wrote down, hopefully. Did everyone just write that down? Did I go too fast over that? I did too fast? Okay. Okay. So, no, no, you're good. So if all you have to do is divide what your total, like you don't need to do each item individually. If you have what your total expenses are, just divide it by 200. So if you have $25,000 in expenses, then dividing it by 200 would be 125, right? And then that would be how many EFTs you have to cover your base. And then depending on what your goals are, I just use the example of $15,000 a month. But whatever your goals are, and if you guys are partners, I just saw Ari Lewis over there. If you guys are partners, then it's like, okay, well, we both want to take home 10 a month. Okay. So then, you know, salaries are covered, but we want to take 10 each in dividends, then you're going to need 20,000 left over. So 100 extra EFTs on top of your payroll base. Did I talk too fast? Okay. Cool. So just add those together, and then that'll give you your real world numbers of what you need to hit. And then what I would recommend you do is you need to live, sleep, and eat that number. It needs to be everywhere. It needs to be on your computer. So does anyone here read Pre-Swasion? My rival, Kail Dini, some people. Okay. So you can, if you've seen in sales rooms where they have conqueror overcome, like one more, all that stuff in sales rooms, they thought it used to be like rah-rah, but when they did research on it, they self-hitted increased productivity by like 30 or 40%, like a massive amount, because what it did was it redirected people's attention on a regular basis. So they're like, you know, they're calling, calling, calling, and then they're like, squirrel. You know what I mean? It's like, oh, one more. Okay. Then they get back. And so what that did was it just kept drawing them back, no matter what they were looking at. And so the more places you can put that number, I know this sounds corny, but this is like, this is what we do. Like, Laila and I are like, we're old school. Like put it on the background of your phones every time you unlock it, you're like, that's my number. Like how close am I? How close am I? How close am I? And you just get obsessed with it. And if you ask R$app, I'm very obsessive. And when you have that, it just makes your life so much easier because you know that nothing else matters. Right? Okay. So put that on the background of your phone. It should be your only focus. Now once you have that number, it's time to figure out when you're going to hit it if nothing improves. So my biggest pet peeve is when people are like, well, I'm going to double my closing rate and I'm going to increase my show rate by like 50%. We're probably going to get leads at half the cost soon. You know, like, let's just work with what is. All right. And so if, based this number that I'm about to show you on just a projection, based on if nothing improves in your life, right? Like the reason we're pretty good at making projections on this stuff is just because I know what our run rates are. I know what percentage people we're converting on, converting from launch to legacy. And I know that if we continue at this rate, then we should be here. Now if I increase the flow, then that's just bonus. That means we just get there faster. At the very least, we should get there at this time, right? And we should be improving until then. So here's the hypothetical math. So you start with the current number. I really hope I'm not like going super like people aren't following along. I'm like, God, I hope not. Okay. So what I'm accounting for, if you think about this from a real standpoint, and there's this Google doc, and when you guys download it, you can click here and see the thing. But what this does is we know what our numbers are. These are the three numbers that we had before. 10 percent attrition rate. We're signing up 37 people a month. And we're starting with 120. So we add these two together. But why isn't it 157? Because we lost 10 percent. Right? We add another 37, but then we lose 10 percent. And you notice that as we move along here, the growth rate slows down because the attrition takes a bigger and bigger chunk out of it. Right? Which is why as you grow in the beginning, how easy is it to go to your first 100 EFTs? Not that hard, right? But then to go to your next 100 EFTs, it's way harder, right? And it's because of this. Because your attrition rate, if you're losing 10 percent and you have 40 people, right, you lose four people. It's one person a week. You're like, I can flip that, right? But if you're at 100 or 150, now you've got to replace 15 people. Just to stay where you're at. Right? So the game gets bigger. And so when I break this down, you just follow that equation and it'll add it up. So something kind of funny for us was, I'll try not to go too far on a tangent, but with Jim Launch, we were doing the same run rate of revenue in June as we basically were like a month or two ago. We just increased our sales team. But until a month ago, we basically had the exact same run rate. The only difference is that the lifetime value of the customer had not stacked yet. It's the only difference. And so when we were in June or August, I think we were doing like 400 or 500,000 a month. And now we're doing 2 million a month in revenue. And the only difference that's changed is just that it's stacked. It's all it is. It's like we still signed up 50 a month. It's just that now we have signed up eight more months of that 50. It's not sexy. It's just like that's all we've done. Does that make sense? Everyone's like, your business is growing at such a crazy rate. It's like not really. It's actually stayed the exact same. It's just the revenue that we should have had has come to fruition. So what are the sales that you have each month or that you need to have each month and you are divided by what your conversion rate on your leads is? Okay? What that does is it tells you how many leads per month you need. So like, I'm going to give a simple example. I should probably use these easier numbers. I wanted to get real numbers, but this probably confused people. Let's say I just wanted to sign up 20 people a month on EFT. So if I convert 20% of my leads to get 20, 20% of what? It was 20. 100. Right? So I need to have four leads a month in order to get 20 EFTs and that's the backwards math. And then if I have 100 leads a month that I need to get, how many leads is that a day? Divide by 30, you have 3.3, so I'd say I need four leads a day to get there. That make sense? Okay. So the first benchmark, there's three that we're going to go over, is how many leads a day do I need? Okay? So this is where you look on your calendar and this is where it takes discipline, put four to six hours a side every week to make new ads. I said four to six hours, not 30 minutes because you're going to have to go record new stuff, you're going to have to put stuff together, you'll have to use Canva and whatever other apps, you know, to put the banner on the video and put the little emoji, wowie, you know, whatever thing, like it just takes time, right? It took me half a day to make all the new ads that we did. It's not like I'm any exception here. But do this every week because then you'll never have the, like, you'll have some that are producing well, some that aren't, but if you have this regular flow of fresh stuff and the key is that it has to look fresh, which means you have to think. Don't do anything else. It's like first rule of investing. You know, don't lose money. Second rule. Don't forget number one. Simple. Okay. Simple, not easy though. Right? I get it. It's not easy. That's why most people don't do it. Now, all you have to do, and this is kind of the next piece and this is where a lot of the event's going to go, is communicate these benchmarks with your team and measure them weekly in your meetings, if not daily. Right? So you should know who left and who came every day. Right? Because that starts the day, everyone sets its own, everyone gets excited, and this is what you're doing. Like, the bigger your business gets and the more successful, whatever you want to say, you get, the more you'll realize that it's not about you. It's not. Like, there's literally so much work to be done for us, like as Jim Launch as a business. I can't do it. So if I actually get in to customer service and start answering questions, the business will suffer. Like if you hop on the floor to go train some sessions, unless you're doing it once every once in a while to reconnect with your client so that you can write better ad copy, like the reason I stay on the day that they call is I like to make sure that everything's still going good. It just gives me peace at night. But whatever that thing is for you, that's okay, but it has to have that purpose. If you're just covering, that's not a good use of your time. Right? And so eventually, it has to become bigger than you and your business will become like a supersuit of what you would be doing at each step of this, because you have your trainers training the way you'd want them to. You'd have your operation manager running the way they should be doing it. And all of them should hopefully be better than you at it. I'll bet my sales team is better at closing as Jim Launch sales than I have. Like I haven't even done it in the last seven, eight, 10 months. I don't know. I don't think I've ever done it. No, I have. Whatever. Anyways, the point is, is that a lot of people will get better because that's all they have to do. Like you have to run your entire business. You're thinking about sales, marketing, like lead you in retention. Like you have all this stuff on your plate. And like if you're like, all I need you to do is get that name to walk in the door and hand us money. That's it. You just do that. And then like once that person walks in the door, like you get them to smile every day. Okay. You know, like it's just, but then they get really good at just doing this one thing over and over again because boring makes you money. And I hope that that becomes like a slogan for your business. And this is also number one for some reason. So in your daily huddles, the first thing that gets brought up is this critical number. You'll notice the repetition here because I'm trying to drive a point home. This way everyone knows what the Super Bowl is and where we're trying to go. That's it. It's team buy-in. Everyone gets it. And you can take a page out of this presentation and repeat it all the time. There's an interview with, shoot, I can't remember which billionaire it was, but he had taken his company from, I think, it was like 10 or 20 million or something up to like two or three billion in sales over an eight year period. And they were like, what was the secret? What was the key that really did it? He's like running a business is a lot like parenting. He's like, we find the six to eight things that matter most to us. And then I repeat them every single day and I have repeated them for the last eight years. And a lot of times it is that and we have as humans and entrepreneurs especially have this need for novelty because it's exciting. But the reality is that it's not going to serve your business. And most of your employees are not like you and if you change stuff all the time, they feel like there's no direction and then they just freeze. They just don't know what to do. Because we're doing that way or going that way. Because in your mind, you're like, oh, this is the next logical conclusion. But in reality, it doesn't seem logical to them at all. So when you have this, it's like you're rallying cry. Sorry, we're breathing into it. Everyone's like, okay, I get that. So my piece of how I contributed to that is that I make sure that people don't leave, which is the bottom part of the number. And then these guys are like, I can make sure that the top number goes up on this fraction. That's what my job is. So that that number always goes up so that we all have jobs. Right? Okay, hopefully I'm breaking this down. That's it. Okay.
